families.label: Adoption lifecycle
families.purpose: Select and assess repository adoption stages.

families.label: CLI metadata and conformance
families.purpose: Expose, route, generate, and self-check CLI contract surfaces.

families.label: Deployment and readiness
families.purpose: Admit deployment evidence and bounded closeout decisions.

families.label: Migration and retirement
families.purpose: Prove parity and plan retirement of consumer-local infrastructure.

families.label: Proof artifact governance
families.purpose: Govern custom rules, document lifecycle, freshness, and witness plans.

families.label: Proof binding topology
families.purpose: Admit, resolve, partition, and inspect requirement proof topology.

families.label: Receipt authority
families.purpose: Admit receipts, producer compatibility, currentness, and trust classes.

families.label: Release artifact consumption
families.purpose: Check package release facts and external or registry consumption.

families.label: Repository facts and scanning
families.purpose: Admit explicit repository facts and bounded policy scans.

families.label: Requirement navigation and rendering
families.purpose: Build and present requirement, proof, and coverage views.

families.label: Requirement source lifecycle
families.purpose: Author, admit, transition, and check requirement source claims.

families.label: Scaffolding and capability capture
families.purpose: Produce candidate adoption structures and capability seeds.

families.label: Selective evidence and obligations
families.purpose: Admit selective evidence and decide proof obligations.

families.label: Selective impact planning
families.purpose: Convert explicit change facts into bounded verification plans.

families.label: Test evidence and coverage input
families.purpose: Admit test evidence and compose coverage inputs.

families.label: Workspace topology and package policy
families.purpose: Model workspace topology, shards, and package runtime policy.

nonClaims: Command families do not change leaf command dispatch or execution authority.
Command families do not infer which command a caller should execute.
Command families group the public command inventory for navigation only.
